scope.purpose: Verify the Matrix style system foundation for compact, readable, mobile-safe user and admin shells.

module-checks.check-5: Reduced-motion smoke: all transform-heavy, flicker-heavy, and scanline-heavy effects are disabled or simplified.
module-checks.check-6: git diff --name-only -- install.sh deploy nginx docker-compose.yml
module-checks.check-7: Phase-53 viewport smoke: auth, dashboard, config, subscription, referrals, settings, reduced-motion dashboard, and desktop user route screenshots.
module-checks.check-8: Phase-54 local static viewport/style smoke: admin login, dashboard, users, devices, MTProto, servers, plans, analytics, reduced-motion markers, compact primitives, and protected route source guards; authenticated screenshots remain clean-deploy follow-up.
module-checks.check-9: Phase-55 premium art-direction smoke: shared premium tokens, no radial/orb backgrounds, logo rules, reduced-motion markers, screenshot artifacts, and protected deploy/install guard.
module-checks.check-10: Phase-56 public/auth smoke and screenshots: compact public landing, logo auth lockups, canonical tariff preview, reduced-motion proof, and protected deploy/install guard.
module-checks.check-11: Phase-59 motion smoke: motion budget tokens, route transitions, feedback/status classes, reduced-motion fallback, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard.
module-checks.check-12: Phase-67 auth/splash smoke: no visible auth card frame, large unframed logo, red focus border, icon-safe inputs, elegant buttons, side-by-side secondary actions, responsive safety, and reduced-motion fallback.
module-checks.check-13: Matrix rain CSS reliability smoke: fallback layer exists in user/admin VisualShell/CSS, canvas and fallback use mobile overscan, reduced-motion keeps a static visible fallback, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard remains clean.
module-checks.check-14: Phase-74 style smoke asserted admin login icon padding, bounded admin rail, content-scroll CSS markers, and no scroll/pointer interception from Matrix layers.

phase-53-style-gates.gate-1: User cabinet routes use Matrix palette/tokens while preserving compact operational layout and avoiding nested cards.
phase-53-style-gates.gate-2: VPN config actions, QR/download/copy, MTProto secrets, countdown/calendar, tariff prices, referral links, and settings forms remain readable.
phase-53-style-gates.gate-3: Focus states remain visible on buttons, links, inputs, segmented controls, copy actions, tariff CTAs, and navigation tabs.
phase-53-style-gates.gate-4: Reduced-motion disables flicker-heavy/transform-heavy effects and keeps the Matrix identity as a static readable surface.
phase-53-style-gates.gate-5: Palette review confirms cyan, magenta, amber, red, and neutral accents remain available and the UI is not a one-note green surface.

phase-54-style-gates.gate-1: Admin console routes use Matrix palette/tokens while preserving dense operational layout and avoiding nested card bloat.
phase-54-style-gates.gate-2: Admin tables, forms, graph panels, MTProto IP history, alert controls, tariff rows, node/route forms, and status badges remain readable.
phase-54-style-gates.gate-3: Focus states remain visible on login inputs, sidebar/mobile navigation, filters, pagination, confirmation buttons, alert buttons, and form controls.
phase-54-style-gates.gate-4: Reduced-motion disables flicker-heavy/transform-heavy effects and keeps the admin shell readable as a static Matrix surface.
phase-54-style-gates.gate-5: Palette review confirms cyan, magenta, amber, red, neutral, and green accents remain balanced so the admin UI is not a one-note green surface.

phase-55-style-gates.gate-1: User and admin CSS expose premium Matrix palette, type, density, shadow, focus, and logo tokens.
phase-55-style-gates.gate-2: Decorative radial/orb shell backgrounds are removed while Matrix identity remains visible.
phase-55-style-gates.gate-3: Reduced-motion fallback simplifies grid/scanline effects and preserves readability.
phase-55-style-gates.gate-4: Phase-55 screenshot artifacts cover phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion login shells.

phase-56-style-gates.gate-1: Public/auth CSS primitives expose compact premium Matrix landing, tariff preview, logo, and auth lockup styles.
phase-56-style-gates.gate-2: Public/auth screenshots cover phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion viewports without visible overlap or hidden CTA.

phase-59-style-gates.gate-1: User and admin CSS expose short motion budget tokens and Matrix route/feedback/status motion classes.
phase-59-style-gates.gate-2: Reduced-motion fallback disables Phase-59 transforms/animations while preserving the static Matrix identity.
phase-59-style-gates.gate-3: CSS smoke rejects scroll hijack, pointer interception, long motion budget, negative letter spacing, viewport font sizing, and protected backend/deploy/runtime diffs.

phase-74-style-gates.gate-1: Admin login icon-bearing inputs must reserve enough padding for icon, placeholder, and typed value across hover/focus/error states.
phase-74-style-gates.gate-2: Admin rail CSS must expose bounded height/overflow markers and must not extend past the usable content height.
phase-74-style-gates.gate-3: Admin content CSS must expose a normal vertical scroll surface so wheel/touch scrolling works over content, with Matrix background layers remaining pointer-safe.
phase-74-style-gates.gate-4: Removing visible Tariffs navigation must not leave empty visual gaps, orphaned active states, or oversized route placeholders.

phase-67-style-gates.gate-1: Auth routes must not render the visible matrix-auth-card frame or framed logo lockup.
phase-67-style-gates.gate-2: Auth input focus/active state must use the planned red treatment and must not regress to blue/cyan focus on /login, /register, /forgot-password, or /reset-password.
phase-67-style-gates.gate-3: Input icons, placeholders, typed values, password controls, and button text must not overlap at Phase-67 phone, tablet, desktop, or reduced-motion viewports.
phase-67-style-gates.gate-4: Primary/secondary auth buttons must remain compact, touch-safe, animated within the motion budget, and disabled/simplified under reduced-motion.

rainfix-style-gates.gate-1: User and admin CSS must expose .matrix-rain-fallback and matrixCssRainFallback.
rainfix-style-gates.gate-2: .matrix-canvas must use 100dvh+128px min-height and normal blend mode so browser-specific blend/viewport behavior cannot hide the rain.
rainfix-style-gates.gate-3: Reduced-motion must leave a static fallback visible rather than removing the Matrix background entirely.
rainfix-style-gates.gate-4: CSS smoke must reject accidental removal of CSS_RAIN_FALLBACK_READY and MOBILE_OVERSCAN_READY markers.

scenarios.scenario-1: Shared Matrix tokens apply to both frontends without breaking compact operational layout rules from M-038.
scenarios.scenario-2: CRT scanlines, neon flicker, and glitch accents are visible but do not obscure forms, tables, proxy cards, tariffs, alerts, or primary actions.
scenarios.scenario-3: Focus states are visible over the Matrix background for links, buttons, inputs, tabs, menus, and confirmation controls.
scenarios.scenario-4: Mobile shells have no document-level horizontal scroll, no nested card bloat, and stable compact control dimensions.
scenarios.scenario-5: Long emails, CTA proxy hostnames, secrets, config labels, tariff prices, and admin row actions wrap or truncate intentionally inside stable containers.
scenarios.scenario-6: Color scan confirms the final theme is not a one-note green surface: cyan, magenta, neutral, warning, and danger accents remain available.

visual-checks.check-3: No status badge, button, table, chart, proxy secret, or tariff text overlap.
visual-checks.check-4: Visible focus states remain clear over Matrix background and scanline overlays.
visual-checks.check-5: Primary VPN, MTProto, tariff, registration, and admin actions are visible and readable.

evidence-levels.module-local: Static smoke validates CSS tokens, class primitives, reduced-motion selectors, focus selectors, and protected-surface guard.
evidence-levels.wave-level: User and admin builds validate shared style integration in both apps.
evidence-levels.phase-level: Viewport screenshots validate readability, no overlap, no horizontal scroll, and balanced Matrix palette.

redaction.rule-1: Visual smoke must not print real tokens, proxy secrets, tg links, VPN config contents, admin tokens, raw user IP history, or screenshot binary data.
redaction.rule-2: Allowed output: route name, viewport, layout metrics, CSS selector presence, screenshot path, and redacted console marker counts.

autonomous-execution-safety.allowed: Implement frontend-only CSS token system, shell classes, focus states, and smoke checks inside M-071 boundaries.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked: Changing data contracts, auth/session storage, billing/VPN/MTProto behavior, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, or backend runtime behavior.
autonomous-execution-safety.handoff-trigger: Stop and report if the theme makes operational content unreadable, creates horizontal scroll, hides primary actions, or conflicts with reduced-motion policy.

acceptance.target-2: Phase-52 smoke passes with token, reduced-motion, viewport, focus, and protected-surface checks.
acceptance.target-3: Mobile and desktop screenshots show Matrix style without text overlap, control overlap, or one-note color dominance.

phase-74-evidence.summary: Admin CSS exposes icon-safe input padding, bounded rail, content overflow-y scroll, and pointer-safe scroll markers without changing backend/deploy/runtime/user frontend surfaces.

local-2026-06-03.command-4: Chrome headless screenshot audit for user/admin /login at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 1440x900, plus reduced-motion 390x844.
local-2026-06-03.viewport-review: Login shells stayed readable, Matrix layer was visible, focus and reduced-motion selectors are present, and no obvious icon/text overlap was observed on captured login viewports.
local-2026-06-03.protected-surface: No install.sh, deploy, nginx, docker-compose, or runtime topology diff.

phase-53-local-2026-06-03.command-3: Chrome production-preview screenshots with virtual-time-budget for /login, /register, /reset-password, desktop /login, and reduced-motion /login.
phase-53-local-2026-06-03.viewport-review: Auth screenshots were readable after final animation frame, no visible input/icon overlap was observed, and Phase-53 static smoke covered protected dashboard/config/subscription/referrals/settings route surfaces that redirect without local auth session.
phase-53-local-2026-06-03.protected-surface: No install.sh, deploy, nginx, docker-compose, or runtime topology diff.

phase-54-verification-design-2026-06-03.summary: Phase-54 style gates require compact Matrix admin route surfaces, readable tables/charts/forms, focus-visible controls, reduced-motion proof, no horizontal overflow, no text/icon overlap, balanced palette, and protected-surface isolation.

phase-54-local-2026-06-03.summary: Static style proof passed for compact Matrix admin primitives, login shell, route markers, bounded lists, chart frames, no oversized login hero/large radius/tracking drift, focus/reduced-motion markers, balanced palette markers, and protected-surface isolation.
phase-54-local-2026-06-03.follow-up: Authenticated screenshot review for protected admin routes remains clean-deploy follow-up.

phase-55-local-2026-06-03.summary: Premium art-direction smoke passed for user/admin shared tokens, no radial/orb backgrounds, compact density, logo rules, reduced-motion policy, screenshot artifact validation, and protected deploy/install guard.

phase-56-local-2026-06-03.command-3: Chrome production-preview screenshots for public/auth routes at phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion viewports.
phase-56-local-2026-06-03.summary: Public/auth style smoke passed for compact landing, visible logo, readable auth forms, canonical tariff preview, reduced-motion markers, and protected deploy/install guard.

phase-59-local-2026-06-04.summary: Phase-59 CSS smoke passed for motion budget, microinteraction/status/route classes, reduced-motion fallback, pointer/focus markers, no scroll hijack, and protected backend/deploy/runtime isolation.

phase-67-local-2026-06-04.command-3: node scripts/phase55-premium-art-direction-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase56-premium-public-auth-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase60-premium-qa-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase62-compactness-deletion-smoke.mjs
phase-67-local-2026-06-04.summary: Phase-67 CSS/auth polish passed for frameless auth surfaces, large unframed logo, red focus treatment, compact secondary actions, mobile wrapping, reduced-motion simplification, and protected backend/deploy/runtime isolation.

rainfix-local-2026-06-04.command-3: node scripts/phase52-matrix-visual-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase59-motion-interactions-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase61-responsive-adaptation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase67-auth-splash-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase60-premium-qa-smoke.mjs
rainfix-local-2026-06-04.summary: CSS fallback and overscan passed static and browser checks; user/admin mobile screenshots show Matrix rain behind content through the bottom edge without visible black cutoff.

V-M-071.phase-68-execution-evidence: 2026-06-04: Phase-68 CSS and route smokes passed for frameless user-shell logo, compact dashboard subscription panel, responsive tariff grid, compact cross-month calendar, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard.

phase-68-verification-design.scope: Matrix style verification for compact dashboard/subscription refactor.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-1: Dashboard, MTProto, subscription, tariff, calendar, referral, and settings controls must keep compact Matrix styling without nested-card bloat.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-2: Buttons must have visible hover/focus/active feedback, red-accent focus where auth style conventions apply, and reduced-motion fallback.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-3: Calendar and tariff controls must not use hero-scale typography, viewport-scaled fonts, negative letter spacing, or oversized radius.

phase-70-verification-design.gate-1: QR presentation may be visually simplified only by changing rendering options, never by changing config payload content.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-2: Removed config diagnostic/raw blocks must not leave empty visual gaps, broken spacers, or hidden actionable content.

V-M-071.phase-70-evidence: 2026-06-04: Phase-70 smoke passed; QR page compaction stayed inside Config.tsx route-local sizing/spacing, with no new global CSS bloat or deploy/runtime drift.

phase-71-verification-design.scope: Matrix style verification for master-detail config UX and requested user-cabinet polish.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-1: Selected-device rows, detail panel, sticky mobile actions, icon-only close, and calendar boundary pulse must remain compact and readable.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-2: User-cabinet logo must not render a square green frame around the transparent PNG.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-3: Reduced-motion must disable or soften flicker/pulse/transform effects while preserving visible state.

V-M-071.phase-71-evidence: Frontend build and Phase-71 smoke passed for Phase-71 CSS markers: master-detail rows, selected row state, sticky actions, icon-only QR close, frameless user logo, calendar boundary pulse, and reduced-motion fallback.

phase-72-verification-design.scope: Matrix style verification for compact mobile bottom navigation, desktop calendar density, and Russian-only Settings cleanup.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-1: Bottom navigation must use compact Matrix styling, visible focus states, safe-area padding, and stable dimensions without nested-card bloat.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-2: Desktop calendar density must shrink through spacing/grid/token changes without viewport-scaled fonts, negative letter spacing, or unreadable date cells.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-3: Removing Settings language controls must not leave blank panels, broken spacing, or orphaned language selector styles visible in user routes.

V-M-071.phase-72-evidence: Phase-72 smoke, Phase-59 motion smoke, and Phase-61 responsive smoke passed for compact bottom dock styling, focus/active states, reduced-motion safety, and smaller desktop subscription calendar density.

phase-73-verification-design.scope: Matrix style verification for compact config copy, Amnezia guidance, and selected-device controls.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-1: "KPN Конфигурация", AmneziaVPN .conf import guidance, AmneziaWG QR action, .conf download, Copy, and device-limit tariff copy must remain readable without icon/text/button overlap.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-2: Removing the selected-device helper sentence must not leave empty layout gaps, hidden actions, or oversized explanatory panels.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-3: Reduced-motion and bottom-dock safe-area rules from Phase-72 must still protect the config route and QR modal.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-4: Phase-73 must not introduce viewport-scaled fonts, negative letter spacing, nested cards, one-note palette drift, or decorative bloat.

phase-73-evidence.summary: KPN copy, AmneziaVPN .conf guidance, selected-device AmneziaWG QR, and tariff-limit copy stay inside existing compact Matrix page/modal primitives with no new global CSS bloat.

phase-74-verification-design.scope: Matrix style verification for admin login icon safety, admin shell scroll repair, and bounded rail geometry.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-1: Phase-74 must not introduce text/icon overlap, viewport-scaled fonts, negative letter spacing, nested cards, or one-note palette drift.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-2: Rail/content scroll fixes must preserve reduced-motion policy and pointer-safe Matrix canvas/fallback layers.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-3: Static CSS smoke is required before claiming browser-safe admin scroll behavior.
